content: David has three sisters. Each of them has one brother. How many brothers does David have?
There are none – David has **no brothers**.
If we change the previous question and assume that each sister of David has two brothers, how many brothers would David have?

Each sister of David has exactly two brothers.  
That means the entire family has **two male children**.  

David himself counts as one of those two brothers, leaving  

\[
\text{Number of brothers David has} = 2\;(\text{total males})-1\;(\text{David}) = 1.
\]

So David has **one brother**.
